summary: Iteration-2 testing complete for Ajo Platform new features (DB-backed Settings, Invitations, Group Comments, group rules_text/enable_comments). 32/32 new pytest tests pass, 21/21 regression pytests still pass (53/53 total). Frontend Playwright covered: admin login, /admin/settings save+persist, create-group modal new fields, AdminGroupDetail Invitations tab create+list, Comments tab post+list, full anonymous /invite/{token} accept flow that registers a new user, joins the group, and redirects to the group page. No bugs found.

issues: Console hydration warning: <span> child inside <option>. Caused by dev-time x-source-* annotation injecting a span into <option> in availableUsers map. Cosmetic only (non-prod), but worth cleaning up.

critical_code_review_comments: settings PUT only $sets non-empty fields, so admins cannot CLEAR a Twilio cred via the UI (sending '' is filtered out at line 669). Consider treating explicit null/empty-string differently from 'not provided'.

send_email failure path returns False but doesn't surface to the admin UI; if Resend domain is not verified the invite UI still shows 'Invitation created and sent' even when delivery failed. Consider returning per-channel failure reason.

/api/invite/{token}/accept performs sign-up + cookie issue inside same handler. Race / partial-failure: if group_members.insert fails after users.insert, user is created but not in group. Wrap in a transaction or compensating delete.
Public /api/invite/{token} exposes group payment_account_details? -> Currently it does NOT (only safe fields), good. But consider also masking due_time/late_fee fields per privacy preference.
Settings collection holds plaintext Twilio auth token + Resend API key in MongoDB. At minimum, add at-rest encryption or use a secrets vault; masking only happens on GET response.
rules_text has no length limit on input - a malicious admin could store arbitrarily large content. Suggest cap at 5-10KB in Pydantic.

Comments body is trimmed to 2000 chars but enable_comments=False does not retroactively hide existing member comments from list_comments; only blocks posting. Probably intended, but worth documenting.
test_credentials: admin@ajo.com / admin123 (super_admin) - works. Members created on the fly via /api/auth/register (TEST_iter2_member_*).
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_RulesGroup_<ts>, TEST_NoComments_<ts> groups; TEST_iter2_member_<ts>, TEST_iter2_out_<ts>, TEST_invitee_<ts>, TEST_revoke_<ts> users; multiple invitations and comments. Settings doc was updated to brand_name=TEST_Brand_<ts>_v2 and Twilio test credentials (ACtest..., tok_test...). NOTE: Twilio creds are dummy strings - real WhatsApp send will fail silently (expected per spec since Twilio.Client init won't actually authenticate). Recommend resetting brand_name and clearing Twilio fields if running in shared env.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two pytest files now: backend_test.py (iter1 regression, 21 tests) and test_iter2_features.py (iter2 new features, 32 tests). Run together: pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. New data-testids added: settings-form/settings-save/settings-msg, invite-create-form/invite-email/invite-phone/invite-submit, gtab-invitations/gtab-comments, comments-section/comment-input/comment-submit, rules-section/invite-form/invite-name/invite-password/invite-accept-rules. Public invite route: /invite/:token works without auth. After accept the new user is auto-logged in and redirected to /groups/{id}. Twilio is intentionally a no-op when creds absent (sent.whatsapp=false). I left brand_name=TEST_Brand_<ts>_v2 in DB; main agent should reset to 'Ajo Platform' before demo if needed.
rca of the issue: N/A - no bugs found in this iteration. Two initial test failures were due to incorrect response-shape expectations in my test script (admin/groups/{id} returns {group, members, cycles}, not flat group). Fixed and re-verified.
